title: Task Parallelization Conservative Scheduler Plan
goal: Add conservative task-lane scheduling constraints and observability without enabling multiple provider jobs.
summary: Extend Phase4 task contracts with resource locks and parallel safety, add scheduler explanation helpers for ready and waiting tasks, and expose those explanations through task-lane summaries while keeping max_parallel_jobs=1 behavior intact.
non_goals: Do not enable official max_parallel_jobs > 1 execution.
Do not introduce a headless worker pool.
Do not run provider execution outside the existing single-step path.
Do not implement same-workspace actual-diff commit guards.
Do not implement isolated workspaces or merge-back.
Do not implement visible worker slot launchers.
Do not treat Phase4 prose parallel groups as canonical scheduler input.
Do not infer same-workspace co-dispatch safety from changed_files alone.
Do not persist ready_queue as a new source of truth; derive it from run-state and task contracts.

implementation_steps.title: Phase4 contract safety fields
implementation_steps.goal: Add optional resource_locks[] and parallel_safety to Phase4 task contracts.
tasks: Validate optional resource_locks as an array of non-empty strings.
Validate optional parallel_safety as serial, cautious, or parallel.
Update Phase4 prompt to request the fields.
Update Phase4-1 prompt to review the fields.
acceptance: phase4_tasks.json tasks[] accepts optional resource_locks as an array of non-empty strings.
phase4_tasks.json tasks[] accepts optional parallel_safety as serial, cautious, or parallel.
Phase4 prompt asks authors to emit these fields.
Phase4-1 prompt asks reviewers to check them.
verification: Run artifact validation tests for valid and invalid Phase4 task safety fields.
Run regression tests that cover legacy Phase4 tasks without the optional fields.

implementation_steps.title: Conservative scheduler constraint helpers
implementation_steps.goal: Add helpers that explain dispatch eligibility without enabling actual parallel execution.

parallel_safety.serial: Cannot be co-dispatched with any other active job; if a serial task is active, no other task is dispatchable.
parallel_safety.cautious: Eligible only when dependencies and resource locks allow it; no extra priority boost in this slice.
parallel_safety.parallel: Eligible when dependencies and resource locks allow it; changed_files alone is not used to infer same-workspace safety in this slice.

resource_lock_blocking.blocked_by: For wait_reason=resource_lock, blocked_by is the list of blocking resource lock ids.

tasks: Represent unmet dependencies as wait_reason=dependencies with blocked_by task ids.
Represent resource locks held by active jobs as wait_reason=resource_lock.
Prevent serial tasks from being dispatchable when another job is active, and prevent other tasks from being dispatchable while a serial task is active.
Represent task_lane.stop_leasing as a stable no-dispatch reason.
acceptance: A task with unmet dependencies reports wait_reason dependencies and blocked_by task ids.
A task with a resource lock held by an active job reports wait_reason resource_lock.
For wait_reason resource_lock, blocked_by contains resource lock ids; helpers may additionally expose blocked_by_jobs and blocked_by_tasks.
A task with parallel_safety serial is not dispatchable when any other job is active, and no other task is dispatchable while a serial task is active.
task_lane.stop_leasing prevents new dispatch and produces a stable wait reason.
Existing sequential Get-NextAction still dispatches the first ready task when no active job exists.
verification: Run targeted scheduler tests for dependencies, resource locks, stop_leasing, and normal sequential dispatch.

implementation_steps.title: Task lane observability
implementation_steps.goal: Expose scheduler explanations in task-lane monitor data.
implementation_steps.relationship_to_prep_plan: Builds on TPP-06 by adding ready_queue, resource_lock waits, serial-safety explanation, and stall reasons. Do not reimplement existing active job display or basic lane counts.
tasks: Include wait_reason and blocked_by in waiting task rows.
Expose lane stall reason such as job_in_progress, stop_leasing, no_ready_tasks, or resource_locked.
Render ready queue and waiting reasons in watch-run.
acceptance: Waiting rows include wait_reason and blocked_by when known.
watch-run can render ready queue and blocked/waiting reasons.
verification: Run targeted summary tests against fixture run states.
Verify with string assertions or a fixture snapshot that rendered monitor data contains ready_queue entries and waiting rows with wait_reason and blocked_by.

implementation_steps.title: Scheduler regression coverage
implementation_steps.goal: Lock in the conservative scheduler foundation before enabling real parallel execution.
tasks: Add tests for optional Phase4 safety fields.
Add tests for resource-lock conflict visibility.
Add tests for stop-leasing stall reason.
Add tests that normal sequential dispatch still works.
acceptance: Targeted scheduler tests cover contract fields, resource locks, stop_leasing, and sequential dispatch.
Regression tests preserve backwards compatibility for Phase4 tasks without optional safety fields.

worker_split.notes: Do not touch scheduler helpers or UI summary files. Parent owns regression.ps1 edits if needed.

Use public helper functions from workflow-engine if present; do not duplicate scheduler policy in renderers. If tests/task-parallelization-scheduler.ps1 already exists, append UI summary assertions in a separate section and avoid editing Worker B's assertions.

global_acceptance: Plans exist as Markdown and JSON under docs/plans.
No production path leases more than one job.
Task-lane state and monitor data can explain ready, dependency wait, resource lock wait, stop-leasing, and active-job capacity states.

llm_execution_guidance.preferred_first_slice: Implement CPS-01, CPS-02, and CPS-03 as separate write scopes, then add or adjust tests through CPS-04.
do_not_do: Do not enable more than one active job.
Do not run provider execution outside the existing sequential step.
Do not rewrite unrelated phase logic.
llm_execution_guidance.verification_policy: Workers may add targeted tests, but the parent agent runs final targeted and full regression checks.
